behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at the module boundary between PR-change discovery and file-selection for analysis. A realistic test wires diff/file-list provider + analyzer selection together and verifies unchanged files are excluded. Rejected System: behavior text is about selection correctness, not real network/OS effects; this can fail with in-process wiring and fixture PR file lists.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline logic with a fixture PR containing changed and unchanged paths (including renames) via a stubbed changed-files provider, execute analyzer selection end-to-end in-process, and assert only changed files are passed to lint execution.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires cross-module handoff; unit-only test would miss integration bugs in file-list plumbing.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is between findings aggregation, severity bucketing, and PR-comment rendering/payload construction. Rejected System: behavior text requires correct grouping in comment content, not real GitHub API behavior; an in-process comment sink catches real grouping failures.
behaviors.test_description: Feed mixed-severity findings from multiple files through aggregator + comment formatter + posting adapter (mock sink), then assert output has correct severity sections and correct finding membership/counts per section.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs several modules wired together; lower-level unit of only bucketing logic would miss formatter/adapter mismatches.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a real CI timeout/performance constraint in GitHub Actions. Real runtime characteristics (process startup, filesystem I/O, scheduling, Action runner limits) can hide in mocks. Rejected Integration: behavior text says 'within the 10-minute Actions timeout,' which is an environment boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the lint step in a CI-like system test (same container/runtime class as GitHub Actions) against a generated 200-file PR-sized corpus, measure wall-clock completion, and fail if end-to-end step exceeds a conservative threshold below 10 minutes.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Timeout correctness depends on real environment performance, so system-level timing is required.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly references 'linter process crashes,' which is subprocess/exit behavior. Real crash handling and continuation semantics are system boundaries; mocks can hide signal/exit-code edge cases. Rejected Integration: concrete text requires true process-failure resilience, not just exception handling in one process.
behaviors.test_description: Run the orchestrator against a fixture set where one file deterministically crashes the real linter subprocess; assert non-zero/crash for that file is isolated, subsequent files are analyzed, and final PR comment/output still includes surviving findings plus crash metadata.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test depends on real subprocess crash semantics; lower level would not reliably expose the failure mode.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears when outputs from multiple rules are merged and normalized before rendering; this is a handoff boundary across rule-result producers and dedup/output modules. Rejected System: behavior text is about dedup semantics in output, not OS/network/subprocess. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model-dependent judgment or multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire two rule result producers that emit overlapping findings (same file/line/message/signature variants) through normalization + dedup + formatter, then assert exactly one finding remains per dedup key in final output.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires multi-module merge path; pure unit of one helper may miss key-canonicalization mismatches across producers.
